options.systemMessage:
You take on the role of the Business Analyst in our agile development team. Your primary responsibility is to craft well-structured, clear, and verifiable user stories.

Instructions:
Format: Always use the standard template: As a <role> I want <feature/function> so that <benefit>

Acceptance Criteria:
Provide 3–5 precise criteria per story, phrased as: Given…, When…, Then…

parameters.content: Call Workflow to create user story

# Description of the workflow

This advanced workflow chains two AI Agents together to automate the Agile requirements process. It takes a raw user idea entered via Open WebUI, generates a structured User Story, and automatically passes that story to a second AI Agent to define verifiable Acceptance Criteria in Gherkin format. The final comprehensive result is returned directly to the chat.

### 1. Receipt of the user request:
* A user sends a chat message via Open WebUI containing a rough idea for a feature (e.g., "I need a forgot password page").
* This request triggers the initial custom N8N webhook. The raw chat message is packaged into a JSON payload and securely transmitted to the N8N pipeline.

### 2. Phase 1 - Generation of the User Story:
* The initiating workflow maps the input and forwards it to the primary AI Agent ("User Story Creator").
* This agent acts as a Business Analyst, analyzing the raw input and formulating a standardized User Story (following the format: ‘As a [role], I want [...] so that [...]’). 
* Instead of returning this story to the user, the N8N workflow captures this structured text to use as the foundation for the next step.

### 3. Phase 2 - Generation of Acceptance Criteria:
* An HTTP POST node dynamically maps the output from Phase 1 and passes the completed User Story directly into a second AI Agent Node ("Gherkin Generator").
* This second agent uses a specialized prompt to extract the intent of the finalized User Story and drafts 3–5 precise rules using the industry-standard Given/When/Then (Gherkin) behavior-driven development format.

### 4. Final Delivery:
* The final Acceptance Criteria are captured by a "Respond to Webhook" node.
* N8N sends the completed, highly-structured Agile requirements back to OpenWebUI, where they are displayed to the user in a single cohesive chat response.
